site (while recommendations are also available in a limited
form to signed out users, we focus on signed in users for

concepts.finding:
An additional primary goal for YouTube
recommendations is to maintain user privacy and provide
explicit control over personalized user data that our back-
end systems expose.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
SYSTEM DESIGN
The overall design of the recommendation system is guided
by the goals and challenges outlined above: We want rec-
ommendations to be reasonably recent and fresh, as well as
diverse and relevant to the user’s recent actions.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
2.5
User Interface
Presentation of recommendations is an important part of
the overall user experience.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The primary metrics we consider
include click through rate (CTR), long CTR (only counting
clicks that led to watches of a substantial fraction of the
video), session length, time until ﬁrst long watch, and rec-
ommendation coverage (the fraction of logged in users with
recommendations).
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
INTRODUCTION
Personalized recommendations are a key method for infor-
mation retrieval and content discovery in today’s information-
rich environment.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Recommendations are fea-
tured in two primary locations: The YouTube home page
(http://www.youtube.com) and the “Browse” page at http:
//www.youtube.com/videos.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
One simple way
to achieve this goal is to impose constraints on the number
of recommendations that are associated with a single seed
video, or by limiting the number of recommendations from
the same channel (uploader).
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
This has
the advantages of allowing the recommendation generation
stage access to large amounts of data with ample amounts of
CPU resources while at the same time allowing the serving
of the pre-generated recommendations to be extremely low
latency.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The actual implementation of YouTube’s recommendation
system can be divided into three main parts: 1) data collec-
tion, 2) recommendation generation and 3) recommendation
serving.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
EVALUATION
In our production system we use live evaluation via A/B
testing [5] as the main method for evaluating the perfor-
mance of the recommendation system.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The advantage of this approach is that
evaluation takes place in the context of the actual website
UI.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
RESULTS
The recommendations feature has been part of the YouTube
homepage for more than a year and has been very successful
in context of our stated goals.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
